preamble: Nasalization of the vowel [a] typically occurs through coarticulation with a following nasal consonant, eventually leading to a phonemic nasal vowel after the loss of the consonant.

examples.note: Evolution from Latin 'manus' where the nasal consonant triggered nasalization of the preceding vowel.
Secondary nasalization of vowels in several Bantu languages due to contact with nasal prefixes.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
